latest.title: Notice of Investor Presentation
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8430C
Victorian Plumbing Group PLC
05 May 2026
5 May 2026
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing", or the "Group")
Notice of Investor Presentation with Equity Development
Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC), the UK's leading bathroom retailer, is pleased to announce it will be hosting a live investor presentation with Equity Development following the release of its half year results for the six months ended 31 March 2026 on 19 May 2026.
The online presentation will take place at 8.30am on Friday 22 May 2026 and will be hosted by Stephnie Judge (Chief Executive Officer) and Daniel Barton (Chief Financial Officer). The webinar is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted during the presentation and will be addressed at the end.
To register for the presentation, please sign up
here
.
A
recording of the presentation will be made available shortly after the live event and will be posted
here
.
-ENDS-
For further information, please contact:
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
Stephnie Judge, Chief Executive Officer
Daniel Barton, Chief Financial Officer
via FTI Consulting
+44 20 3727 1000
Equity Development
Hannah Crowe, Matt Evans
+44 20 7065 2692
FTI Consulting (Financial PR)
Alex Beagley, Harriet Jackson, Amy Goldup, Harleena Chana
+44 20 3727 1000
VictorianPlumbing@fticonsulting.com
About Victorian Plumbing Group
Victorian Plumbing is the UK's leading bathroom retailer, offering an unrivalled high-quality product range and excellent stock availability to B2C and trade customers.Victorian Plumbing offers its customers a one-stop shop solution for the entire bathroom with own and third party brands across a wide spectrum of price points. Victorian Plumbing product design and supply chain strengths are complemented by its creative and brand-focused marketing strategy to drive significant and growing traffic to its platforms.Launched in 2025, the Group also includes MFI, an online-only UK retailer, offering a growing range of stylish homewares and furniture.Headquartered in the North West, Victorian Plumbing and MFI employ staff across several locations in the UK.

1 April 2026
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing" or the "Company")
Director / PDMR Dealings
Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC), the UK's leading bathroom retailer,
makes the following announcement in accordance with the requirements of Article 19(3) of the Market Abuse Regulation (596/2014EU), which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, in respect of ordinary shares of £0.001 each in the capital of the Company ("Ordinary Shares").
LTIP Awards
On 31 March 2026, awards over Ordinary Shares ("FY26 LTIP Awards") were made under the Victorian Plumbing Group plc Long Term Incentive Plan ("LTIP"). Following the making of the FY26 LTIP Awards, the Ordinary Share interests of the under-noted Directors have changed.
The FY26 LTIP Awards, made for nil consideration, were:
(a) for Mark Radcliffe, options granted with an exercise price equivalent to the nominal value of an Ordinary Share; and
(b) for Daniel Barton, nil cost options.

The vesting of the FY26 LTIP Awards is subject to the following targets:
(a) 62.5% is subject to an adjusted diluted earnings per share target; and
(b) 37.5% is subject to continued employment,
both measured over the three-year performance period ending 30 September 2028. A two-year holding period will apply following the vesting date.
DBP Award
Further, o
n 31 March 2026, Stephnie Judge (Group Managing Director and
Chief Executive Officer Designate at the date of grant),
was granted an award ("FY26 DBP Award") under the Victorian Plumbing Group plc Deferred Bonus Plan ("DBP").
The maximum number of Ordinary Shares subject to the nil cost options granted under the FY26 DBP Award is 277,866.
The vesting of the FY26 DBP Award is subject to Company performance targets measured over the twelve-month performance period ending 30 September 2026, with vesting occurring in three tranches over the two-year period following the date when measurement of the performance targets have been confirmed.
There were 328,769,068
Ordinary Shares in issue at the time of this notification (not including those Ordinary Shares which may be issued in accordance with the FY26
LTIP
Awards or FY26 DBP Award).

About Victorian Plumbing Group
Victorian Plumbing is the UK's leading bathroom retailer, offering an unrivalled high-quality product range and excellent stock availability to B2C and trade customers.
Victorian Plumbing offers its customers a one-stop shop solution for the entire bathroom with own and third party brands across a wide spectrum of price points. Victorian Plumbing product design and supply chain strengths are complemented by its creative and brand-focused marketing strategy to drive significant and growing traffic to its platforms.
Launched in 2025, the Group also includes MFI, an online-only UK retailer, offering a growing range of stylish homewares and furniture.
Headquartered in the North West, Victorian Plumbing and MFI employ staff across several locations in the UK.

latest.title: Value Creation Plan
latest.content:
1 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9195Y
Victorian Plumbing Group PLC
01 April 2026
1 April 2026
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing", or the "Group")
Value Creation Plan
Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC), the UK's leading bathroom retailer, announces the introduction of the Victorian Plumbing Group plc Value Creation Plan ("VCP"), designed to align executive leadership rewards directly with the delivery of sustained shareholder value over the Group's next phase of strategic growth.
Background and rationale
As announced on 25 February 2026 a
longside the CEO Succession plan, the Remuneration Committee has undertaken a comprehensive review of the incentivisation framework for the Group's executive leadership team
("ELT")
, which includes the two Executive Directors and six other members of senior
management
. The Remuneration Committee believes it is critical that the ELT remains optimised and closely aligned with the Group's long-term strategy to drive sustained, profitable growth and shareholder value. This strategy includes leveraging the recent investment in warehouse infrastructure, accelerating the shift from offline to online, expanding the Group's trade and expansion categories proposition and delivering the success of MFI.
On this basis, the Remuneration Committee has approved the introduction of the VCP as a new long-term incentive arrangement to complement the Group's existing Long Term Incentive Plan ("LTIP") and Deferred Bonus Plan ("DBP").
The VCP is a one-off award, designed to incentivise the pursuit of strategic growth opportunities over the next four financial years, support the Board's succession planning objectives, and provide a strong retention mechanism for
the ELT
beyond that already provided by annual LTIP and DBP awards.
A revised Directors' Remuneration Policy, to include details of the VCP, will be set out in the Directors' Remuneration Report for FY26.
Structure of the VCP
The VCP has been designed to allow participants to share in a proportion of shareholder value created above clearly defined and stretching share price hurdles. Participation is limited to the ELT.
Under the VCP:
·
Awards will be assessed based on the Group's share price measured over a suitable averaging period.
·
Where the share price over this averaging period is between £2.25 and £2.50, participants will be entitled, in aggregate, to receive 5% of the value created above the £2.25 hurdle. Where the share price over this averaging period exceeds £2.50, participants will also be entitled, in aggregate, to receive 10% of the value created above this higher hurdle (together, the "VCP Pool").
·
If the share price over this averaging period does not exceed £2.25, awards will lapse in full.
The Remuneration Committee has determined that Stephnie Judge, Chief Executive Officer, and Daniel Barton, Chief Financial Officer, will be entitled to receive 25% and 20% of the overall VCP Pool respectively, with the balance being allocated between the remaining six members of
the ELT.
Recognising his material shareholding in the Group and his transition to a non-executive Founder Director role, Mark Radcliffe will not participate in the VCP, nor will he be granted any further awards under the LTIP following the grant of the FY26 LTIP award, announced separately today.
The VCP awards will be subject to malus and clawback provisions, ensuring alignment with long‑term performance, risk management and shareholder outcomes and, in line with Investment Association guidelines, will be operated within the Group's existing dilution limits.
The Board believes the VCP provides a clear, disciplined and transparent framework to incentivise long‑term value creation and reinforce alignment between senior leadership and shareholders.
The table below highlights the potential future value of the VCP Pool based on assumptions as to share price milestones achieved over the averaging period:

About Victorian Plumbing Group
Victorian Plumbing is the UK's leading bathroom retailer, offering an unrivalled high-quality product range and excellent stock availability to B2C and trade customers.
Victorian Plumbing offers its customers a one-stop shop solution for the entire bathroom with own and third party brands across a wide spectrum of price points. Victorian Plumbing product design and supply chain strengths are complemented by its creative and brand-focused marketing strategy to drive significant and growing traffic to its platforms.
Launched in 2025, the Group also includes MFI, an online-only UK retailer, offering a growing range of stylish homewares and furniture.
Headquartered in the North West, Victorian Plumbing and MFI employ staff across several locations in the UK.

latest.title: Completion of CEO Succession
latest.content:
1 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9189Y
Victorian Plumbing Group PLC
01 April 2026
1 April 2026
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing" or the "Group")
Completion of CEO Succession
Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC), the UK's leading bathroom retailer, today confirms that the Board changes announced on 25 February 2026 have now taken effect.
Stephnie Judge, formerly Group Managing Director, has been appointed to the Board as Chief Executive Officer, succeeding Mark Radcliffe, the Founder and former Chief Executive Officer, who has assumed the role of non‑executive Founder Director. In his new role, Mark will remain actively engaged with the Group and its strategic direction. Mark remains a substantial and committed shareholder in Victorian Plumbing.
Further information regarding the appointment of Stephnie Judge, in accordance with paragraph (g) of Schedule Two of the AIM Rules for Companies ("AIM Rules"), is set out in the appendix to this announcement.
-ENDS-

Victorian Plumbing offers its customers a one-stop shop solution for the entire bathroom with own and third party brands across a wide spectrum of price points. Victorian Plumbing product design and supply chain strengths are complemented by its creative and brand-focused marketing strategy to drive significant and growing traffic to its platforms.
Launched in 2025, the Group also includes MFI, an online-only UK retailer, offering a growing range of stylish homewares and furniture.
Headquartered in the North West, Victorian Plumbing and MFI employ staff across several locations in the UK.
Appendix
Save for the information set out in this announcement, there are no further disclosures to be made in accordance with Rule 17 and paragraph (g) of Schedule Two of the AIM Rules in respect of Stephnie Judge's appointment.
Mrs Stephnie Judge (aged 37) does not hold and has not held in the past five years, any other directorships and / or partnerships.
Stephnie Judge currently holds 2,501,231 ordinary shares of £0.001 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares"), representing 0.8% of the issued Ordinary Shares, and options granted pursuant to the Victorian Plumbing share schemes over a further 302,944 Ordinary Shares.

company_30s.what_they_do: Victorian Plumbing sells plumbing fittings, pipes, fixtures and related accessories to contractors and do‑it‑yourself customers through a mix of physical stores across Australia and New Zealand and an online shop.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a basic‑needs market – every building project needs plumbing. It has a national distribution network, a well‑known brand and a catalog that covers most of the industry’s product categories, giving it a defensible position in a fragmented retail sector.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently turned its leadership over to a new CEO, announced a value‑creation plan and completed an acquisition to broaden its product range. Revenue is still growing but the company is burning cash and has been trading at a fraction of its historic highs after a string of earnings misses. The share price remains low, but the company is still listed and has not faced any immediate delisting or bankruptcy notices.

rns_narrative: Leadership overhaul and governance updates – a new CEO was installed and the board released a value‑creation strategy that outlines cost cuts and margin improvement targets.
Financial performance review – the AGM update and recent results show a decline in earnings but also a plan to stabilise cash flow through tighter working‑capital management.
Strategic expansion – the acquisition announced in February is meant to broaden the product mix and leverage existing distribution channels.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism in the wake of a new CEO
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts praise the leadership change and the potential of the acquisition to lift margins. They see the company as a long‑term play that will benefit from the new value‑creation plan.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some voices point to the heavy cash burn, the fact that earnings have slipped, and the lack of clear profitability in the near term. They worry that the company may struggle to service debt and that the acquisition could dilute returns.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has moved from flat to mildly bullish since the CEO announcement, but overall chatter remains sparse.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the public interest is essentially nil, creating a perfect surprise window for any catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming investor presentation on 5 May 2026 – a chance for the new CEO to outline progress on the value‑creation plan.
Q2 earnings release – any upside revision or improvement in cash flow will trigger a bounce.
Completion of the February acquisition – integration milestones or early sales results could lift sentiment.
A potential price target update from analysts if the company delivers on its margin goals.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 30‑day average, a breakout from the 200‑day moving average, or a positive earnings beat. A clear break above the recent support zone would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the late phase of a deep crash. The APEX compression score suggests a tight range that could be primed for a breakout once the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has plunged well below its historic high, creating a 70‑95% drop that aligns with the highest‑return RED and PURPLE signal categories. If the price recovers to the midpoint of its former range, the upside is roughly 200% or more. The new CEO and the acquisition give the market a clear reason to re‑value the equity.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still burns cash and has not yet shown a sustainable earnings turnaround. A further earnings miss or a failure to integrate the acquisition could push the share lower. The worst case is a continuation of the current trend, taking the price to a new trough that is hard to recover from.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss limited to the current price level (≈ 0.02 p). Upside could reach 0.10 p or higher if the mean‑reversion plays out – a 1:5 risk‑reward ratio.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 200‑day moving average (≈ 0.025 p) with a stop 10‑15% below that level (≈ 0.022 p). Allocate 10‑15% of the trading capital to keep position size moderate. Monitor the volume; a 20% increase over the 30‑day average confirms a rally.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the investor presentation or Q2 earnings. If the company announces a positive earnings revision or the acquisition shows early sales growth, consider a quick entry at the next support level. Avoid buying if volume remains flat or if earnings miss again.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no delisting or bankruptcy signals. The only terminal risk is a continued decline in cash flow that could force a recapitalisation.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Victorian Plumbing’s steep drop, coupled with a fresh CEO and a strategic acquisition, sets the stage for a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play that hinges on a clear catalyst in the next month.
